details.body: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5126-5184 still runs the unjournaled exact-title/rendered-description recovery path before the proposal threshold check at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5186-5210. A below-threshold propose_new_bead input can therefore add a dependency and return/record Created whenever a matching bead exists, instead of emitting propose_new_bead_pending with no mutation. That same recovery branch calls record_proposed_bead_created_event at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5174-5182, which omits the new threshold_count and existing_bead_lookup_ran metadata. Gate this recovery path behind the threshold, or require proof of a prior threshold-compliant creation, and emit the structured created event with the policy metadata.

src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4486-4488 loads parsimonious_bead_creation with project_id=None, and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5186-5220 uses that policy for enabled, proposal_threshold, and match threshold decisions. Project-level workflow.parsimonious_bead_creation settings are silently ignored, so a project can set enabled=false or proposal_threshold=3 and this handler will still use workspace/default values. Pass the project id into handle_propose_new_bead or otherwise load the same project-scoped EffectiveConfig used by reconciliation.

The policy check is not consistently applied in `handle_propose_new_bead`. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5188-5201`, the function returns `Pending` as soon as `current_count < threshold`, so it never executes the existing-bead ownership lookup at `:5215+` on below-threshold proposals. This means a strong match to an already-open owning bead can be missed (no comment/de-duplication is recorded) and only a pending event is emitted, which violates the intended order of mapping existing first and then creating new beads.

src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4555 excludes the active bead from the similarity ownership lookup, and the handler later proceeds to create a new bead at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5310 when no other match is found. That skips the required “fix-current-bead first” policy: a propose_new_bead whose title/scope strongly matches the active bead will still create a duplicate follow-up instead of being treated as in-scope current work. Add an active-bead ownership check before creation and block/downgrade creation when the current bead owns the proposed work.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1471 returns below-threshold propose_new_bead groups without emitting any pending journal event. The new propose_new_bead_pending event is only in handle_propose_new_bead, while the terminal success reconciliation path called at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:308 uses reconcile_proposed_bead_records and silently records only amendments_processed. Below-threshold proposals therefore miss the required structured pending event with current count and threshold.

src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5580 suppresses covered-by-existing comments using only classification, active_bead_id, and run_id. If one run maps two distinct propose_new_bead findings from the same active bead to the same existing bead, the second comment is skipped even when its finding summary/proposed scope differ, losing the audit trail for that downgrade. Include a stable finding/amendment marker or compare the finding/scope before treating the comment as already posted.

src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5148-5171 treats a strong match against the active bead as `CoveredByExistingBead` and returns without creating a bead, posting a comment, or routing the finding back as fix-current work. That violates the required policy order: active-bead-owned findings must be fixed on the current bead, not silently marked covered. This can drop a real reviewer finding whenever the similarity check matches the current bead.

src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5133-5145 queries all beads before loading the parsimonious policy and threshold. With `workflow.parsimonious_bead_creation.enabled=false` and `proposal_count < threshold`, threshold enforcement should be able to record a pending event without any existing-bead lookup, but this code can fail on `br list --all` before reaching the pending path at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5176-5221. Load the policy first and short-circuit disabled below-threshold proposals before any bead listing.

Pending journal events are appended unconditionally, so reconciliation is not idempotent for below-threshold proposals. `reconcile_proposed_bead_records` calls `record_propose_new_bead_pending_event` on every pass at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1564-1573, and that helper always appends at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1356-1362. The direct handler has the same pattern at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5212-5221 and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5552-5611. Re-running terminal sync or replaying the same below-threshold proposal will keep adding duplicate `propose_new_bead_pending` entries; dedupe by sub_type, run_id, active_bead_id, amendment/proposal identity, current_count, and threshold before appending.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1575`-`1714` still routes terminal `propose_new_bead` findings by grouping summaries and appending `proposed-beads.ndjson`; it never runs the new open-bead similarity lookup or downgrades to `covered_by_existing_bead`. The production entrypoint calls this path directly at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1780`, so an accepted final-review proposal with a strong existing open bead match will still be surfaced as a new bead proposal once the threshold is met. This bypasses the parsimonious policy implemented in `handle_propose_new_bead` and can create duplicate follow-up work from the real reconciliation flow.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:269 closes the active bead before terminal review-classification reconciliation runs at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:313, but load_active_bead_match only treats the active bead as owned when br list still reports Open/InProgress at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1366. On the normal success path the active bead has just been closed, so a threshold-met propose_new_bead that actually belongs to the current bead bypasses the fix-current-bead-first policy and can be recorded as follow-up work. Include the active bead in the ownership check regardless of terminal status, or run the active-bead ownership check before closing it.

Threshold-met terminal propose_new_bead groups with no existing match only append ProposedBeadRecord rows to proposed-beads.ndjson at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1957 and write that file at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1981. This path never appends a milestone journal event with sub_type propose_new_bead_created, even though CLI/daemon success reconciliation invokes this terminal path at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:313. Add the required structured created event with proposed title/scope/severity, threshold_count, and existing_bead_lookup_ran when the threshold is met.

Terminal final-review reconciliation discards the richer propose-new-bead metadata that final_review already preserves. TerminalReviewFinding has only proposed_bead_summary at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1059, from_final_review copies no proposed_title/proposed_scope/severity/rationale at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1070, TerminalProposeNewBeadInput then uses summary/body text instead at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1291, and pending events hard-code severity to unknown at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1566. Existing-bead matching and journal/proposal records should use the authoritative proposed_title, proposed_scope, severity, and rationale from the accepted final-review amendment when available.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1999` groups propose-new-bead amendments only by normalized proposal title, and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:2012` sums each finding's `occurrence_count` to decide whether the N=2 threshold is met. Because review-stage findings are assigned `occurrence_count: 1` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1091`, a single reviewer/payload can emit two duplicate findings with the same proposed title and incorrectly satisfy the two-source creation threshold. The same double-counting can happen in final review if one source contributes multiple accepted amendments with the same proposed title. This violates the required "proposed by at least 2 sources" policy and can still create/propose beads from one noisy source. Carry a stable source identity per finding and threshold on distinct sources per proposal group instead of summed occurrences.

src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5212 and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5310 still perform pre-create bead discovery in the threshold-met path when workflow.parsimonious_bead_creation.enabled=false. The disabled-policy branch only short-circuits below-threshold proposals; once current_count >= threshold, handle_propose_new_bead calls br list, derives matching_beads, and can reuse an exact title/rendered-description match before create. That means the feature flag does not fully satisfy the acceptance case that disabled policy skips existing-bead lookup while still enforcing the threshold, and a br list/show failure can block creation even though the lookup policy is off.
